serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of fresh milk is 100ml, often used as an addition to beverages or cereals.

nutrient_source.title: FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Food 170787 (Milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat, with added vitamin D) to align with input values where reasonable, and supplemented for missing values.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium, rich in calcium and potassium
hypertension.guidance: Fresh milk is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its low sodium content and beneficial minerals like calcium and potassium, which support blood pressure regulation as part of a balanced DASH diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index, natural sugars, protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fresh milk has a low glycemic index (GI 27) and its sugars are naturally occurring lactose, not added sugars. The protein content helps moderate postprandial glucose response, making it suitable in moderation for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While fresh milk provides beneficial nutrients, individuals with cardiovascular disease should opt for low-fat or skimmed versions to reduce saturated fat intake, which can impact cholesterol levels.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Calcium and protein for bone health
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Fresh milk provides essential calcium and protein, which are important for bone protection in RA patients, especially those on corticosteroids. Some individuals may experience dairy sensitivity, so monitoring personal response is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fresh milk is a nutrient-rich dairy product, typically from cows, known for its creamy texture and mild, slightly sweet taste. It is consumed chilled, either on its own or as an ingredient in various beverages and dishes. Nutritionally, it is a good source of high-quality protein, calcium, and several vitamins (e.g., B12, D), contributing to bone health and muscle maintenance. Its natural sugars are primarily lactose, which has a low glycemic index. For clinical relevance, its low sodium and high calcium/potassium content make it suitable for hypertension, while its low GI and protein content benefit individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderation. Individuals with cardiovascular disease may prefer low-fat options due to saturated fat content. It is a common allergen (milk) but is vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free.
common_user_queries: is fresh milk good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with standard fresh milk compositions from approved sources, with minor adaptations to match input where reasonable. GI is from an approved source.
